summary: Merged a small dtk-template update into the project tooling. The visible code change updates tools/project.py progress reporting to include a new fuzzy_match_percent metric alongside exact matched and linked percentages. The PR body also notes a README.md merge conflict was resolved by keeping upstream's deleted README because the template README was generic and did not apply to Melee.
what_changed: In tools/project.py, print_category now reads fuzzy_match_percent from the measures dictionary with a default of 0.
The category progress line changed from reporting only matched and linked percentages to reporting fuzzy, matched, and linked percentages.
The PR body states this merged latest changes from encounter/dtk-template template/main into master.
The PR body states a README.md conflict was resolved by keeping upstream's deleted README rather than importing the template's generic README.
smart_moves: Used measures.get("fuzzy_match_percent", 0), preserving compatibility with measure dictionaries that do not provide the new field.
Added fuzzy-match reporting without changing the existing matched_code_percent and complete_code_percent fields, so existing exact-match and linked-progress semantics remain visible.
Resolved the README.md conflict by preserving the project-specific upstream state instead of accepting a generic template README that would not apply to the Melee repository.
decomp_lessons: Progress tooling can distinguish fuzzy matching from exact matching; future reporting changes should keep those metrics separate to avoid overstating true matched code.
Template sync PRs may include project-specific conflict resolutions; document those resolutions clearly in the PR body so future syncs know which template files should not be blindly adopted.
When adding new metrics to project reporting, default missing values to 0 to avoid breaking older or partial measurement data.
naming_conventions: Metric keys in tools/project.py use snake_case strings such as total_code, matched_code_percent, fuzzy_match_percent, complete_units, and total_units.
The displayed progress text uses user-facing labels "fuzzy", "matched", and "linked" to distinguish fuzzy match percentage, exact matched percentage, and complete linked percentage.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: No assembly-level matching tactic is shown in this PR. The only matching-related change is tooling output that surfaces fuzzy_match_percent in progress reports.
review_feedback: No review comments were present in the provided PR slice.
The PR body explicitly records the README.md conflict resolution: keep upstream's deleted README because the template's generic README does not apply.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that modify fuzzy_match_percent or progress reporting in tools/project.py.
Check whether fuzzy_match_percent is produced by the measurement pipeline and how it differs from matched_code_percent.
Review future dtk-template merge PRs for repeated README.md conflict handling.
